message:
fix(sync,io,scheduler): correctness fixes across concurrency primitives

Notify — major refactor for cancellation safety:
- Extract pass_baton() helper to consolidate notify-one waker handoff
  logic, ensuring the baton is never lost when a notified future is
  dropped between receiving a notification and being polled
- Decompose monolithic poll() into poll_init/poll_waiting/mark_done
  helpers for clearer state machine transitions
- Add lock-free fast paths for stored notification consumption and
  generation-bump detection before acquiring the waiters lock
- Add regression test (tests/notify_bug.rs) for cancellation safety

OnceCell — handle spurious CAS failures:
- Add Err(UNINIT) match arms in get_or_init, get_or_init_blocking,
  and get_or_init_async to retry on spurious compare_exchange_weak
  failures instead of hitting unreachable!() on weakly-ordered archs

RwLock — add regression test (tests/rwlock_barge_bug.rs) for writer
fairness to prevent reader barging under contention

Pool — precision wake improvements:
- Replace broadcast-wake-all on resource return with targeted single
  waker wake based on available capacity position in the waiter queue
- Thread waiter_id through try_get_idle() and reserve_create_slot()
  so the acquiring waiter is removed from the wait queue atomically
  under the state lock, preventing double-wake
- Process returns in CreateSlotReservation::drop for prompt cleanup

Broadcast channel — clear buffer when last receiver drops:
- When fetch_sub returns 1 (last receiver), lock inner and clear the
  buffer under double-check to reclaim memory

MPSC channel — fix Reserve::drop spurious wake:
- Only wake the next sender when the dropped reservation was the head
  (is_head check), preventing thundering herd on dropped non-head
  reservations

IO copy — flush pending writes when reader is not ready:
- Add need_flush flag to Copy, CopyBuf, and CopyWithProgress futures
- When poll_read returns Pending and data has been written, issue
  poll_flush to ensure partially-written data reaches the destination
  rather than sitting in the writer's buffer indefinitely

IO driver — drop guard before waking:
- Move drop(guard) before waker.wake() calls to avoid holding the
  lock while executing potentially expensive wake callbacks

Scheduler global injector — use atomic counter for readiness checks:
- Replace is_empty/has_ready_work/has_runnable_work calls to
  ready_queue.is_empty() with ready_count.load() to close the race
  window where the counter is incremented but the queue push hasn't
  completed yet, preventing false-empty returns
- Add regression test for counter-visible-but-not-yet-pushed state

Three-lane scheduler — remove unused combined local poll method
that was superseded by the per-lane try_*_work methods

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
